name: Les Gabares Departmental Circuit
name_original: Promenade le long de la Garonne
description: Hike the 4.9-mile Les Gabares Departmental Circuit, a moderate route near Barie featuring the Garonne river and cornfields.
region_intro: Hiking around Barie offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, canals, and historic sites. The region features paths alongside waterways like the Canal latéral à la Garonne, providing flat terrain suitable for various hiking abilities. Trails often pass through agricultural areas and near historic châteaux, reflecting the area's rich heritage. Best hiking trails in Barie The most popular hiking route is Les Gabares Departmental Circuit, a 4.9 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete, offering views of the local riverine landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castets Lock, no. 53 – Tour de Barie 33190. loop from Castets-en-Dorthe, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route follows the canal and passes by historic locks. Local hikers also love the Château de La Réole loop from La Réole, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through the local countryside and past the Château de La Réole, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Barie is defined by river paths, canal-side trails, and routes connecting historic villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging distances. The routes in Barie are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore Barie's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Barie?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes around Barie, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore the region's river valleys, canals, and historic sites.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Barie?
A: Hiking in Barie offers diverse scenery, primarily characterized by river valleys, canals, and agricultural areas. Many paths follow waterways like the Garonne Lateral Canal, providing flat terrain and views of historic locks and châteaux. You'll experience a blend of natural beauty and historical charm.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Barie?
A: Yes, Barie offers several easy trails perfect for families. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour de Barie 33190. loop from Barie&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy route that allows you to enjoy the local surroundings without significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Barie?
A: Yes, many trails in Barie are designed as loops, offering convenient circular walks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e838312090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Gabares Departmental Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de La Réole loop from La Réole&lt;/a&gt; also provides a scenic loop through the countryside.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the hiking trails in Barie?
A: While hiking in Barie, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, with trails often running alongside it, passing historic structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassanne Lock&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historic châteaux, such as the one featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de La Réole loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Barie?
A: The hiking trails in Barie are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful paths along the canals, the charming historic sites, and the varied terrain that makes for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Barie?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging experience, Barie offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29372979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal latéral à la Garonne – Castets Lock, no. 53 loop from Blaignac&lt;/a&gt;. This longer route provides a more demanding hike through the region's landscapes.

Q: Can I hike along the Canal latéral à la Garonne in Barie?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature of the hiking network in Barie. Many trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castets Lock, no. 53 – Tour de Barie 33190. loop from Castets-en-Dorthe&lt;/a&gt;, follow the canal, offering flat and scenic paths past locks and waterways.

Q: What is the typical duration for a hike in Barie?
A: Hikes in Barie vary in duration depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e838312090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Gabares Departmental Circuit&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours, while moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de La Réole loop&lt;/a&gt; typically take about 2 hours and 35 minutes. Longer, more difficult trails will naturally require more time.

Q: Are there opportunities to see historic châteaux while hiking in Barie?
A: Yes, the region around Barie is rich in history, and some hiking routes will lead you past historic châteaux. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15104139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de La Réole loop from La Réole&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering views of the local countryside and the Château de La Réole itself.

Q: What is the best way to find a hiking trail that suits my fitness level in Barie?
A: Barie offers a range of trails classified by difficulty: easy, moderate, and difficult. You can choose from 10 easy, 19 moderate, and 6 difficult routes. This allows you to select a trail that matches your fitness and experience level, ensuring an enjoyable hike.
tips: Short walk, tranquility guaranteed. Can be pleasantly extended by crossing the small village of Barie.

faqs.question: What is the nature of the "Les Gabares Departmental Circuit"?
faqs.answer: The "Les Gabares Departmental Circuit" is listed as a hike on komoot, covering approximately 8 kilometers with minimal elevation gain. However, the name "Gabares" is more commonly associated with traditional flat-bottomed boats that offer scenic river cruises, particularly on the Dordogne River. This circuit likely takes its name from this local heritage, offering a walk that may run alongside or near areas historically significant for gabare activity.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circuit?
faqs.answer: The circuit is estimated to take around 2 hours to complete, making it a manageable outing for most hikers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty level of the hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat, with only about 10 meters of elevation gain and loss. Despite the minimal elevation, the circuit is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty, which might be due to its length or specific ground conditions along the route. It's suitable for those looking for a relatively easy walk without significant climbs.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes points of interest such as the Tour de Barie 33190 and a Cornfield loop on the Garonne, offering scenic views and local landmarks.

faqs.question: Is the circuit dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this circuit are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in France, often on a leash. It's always best to keep your dog under control to respect wildlife and other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: Given the flat terrain and potential for river views, spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if visiting during peak season.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this circuit?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the "Les Gabares Departmental Circuit" as it is a public departmental circuit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the circuit?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact circuit is not provided, but as a departmental circuit, it typically has accessible starting points in or near local villages. Look for public parking areas in nearby towns or designated trailheads.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Les Eaux Mêlées : boucle locale and Les Gabares : circuit départemental, and partially overlaps with L'Imbarradys : boucle locale, Chemin d'exploitation, and V.C. du Port.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate, 2-hour hike, we recommend bringing comfortable walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light snack. A camera would also be great for capturing the scenic views.
